grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 344, 341; taxonomy L&E Document Review and wage labels
grounding_note: Employment intakes split on what the user actually holds and wants: a severance agreement awaiting review (Document Review/Severance), unpaid wages under state law (Wage and Hour) vs federal overtime (FLSA), union grievance machinery, or public-employee civil-service process. Row 344 mixed wage discrepancies into a harassment complaint. All employee-side; the user's role is never the hidden fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm being pushed out of my job and they gave me until Friday to make a decision.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Document Review/Severance Packages - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: They handed me a 14-page severance agreement with a release of all claims — I need it reviewed before Friday's deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They handed me a 14-page severance with a full release — I need it reviewed before Friday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a severance agreement awaits review
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no paperwork — the 'decision' is resign or be fired, right after I refused to falsify safety logs.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No paperwork — it's resign-or-be-fired, right after I refused to falsify the safety logs.
scenarios.vetting_note: Severance-in-hand is a distinct time-boxed service; 'pushed out' reads as wrongful discharge without it.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer owes me money and keeps promising 'next paycheck' that never comes.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee || Labor & Employment > FLSA - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's overtime — they classified all of us 'assistant managers' as exempt while we stock shelves 55 hours a week; I'm told that's a federal FLSA misclassification case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's overtime — they call us exempt 'assistant managers' while we stock shelves 55 hours a week. I'm told that's federal FLSA misclassification.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the claim is federal overtime misclassification or state wage law
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my last two regular paychecks plus unpaid rest breaks — state wage-claim territory.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's two regular paychecks and skipped rest breaks — state wage stuff.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exemption-misclassification vs straight unpaid wages splits the federal/state labels.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm having serious problems with how I'm being treated at work and HR is useless.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > General - Employee || Labor & Employment > Union Issues - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm in the union, and the bigger problem is my own union — they refused to take my grievance to arbitration without explanation, which I'm told may breach their duty to me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm union — and honestly the union's the problem too. They dropped my grievance without explanation, which apparently may breach their duty to me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether union membership and the union's own handling are at issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No union at my shop — it's just me against a manager who rewrites my timecards and schedules me into impossible shifts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No union here — just me against a manager rewriting timecards and impossible schedules.
scenarios.vetting_note: Duty-of-fair-representation claims have their own route; union status is the gateway fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I was disciplined at my job unfairly and I want to fight it through every channel I have.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > General - Employee || Labor & Employment > Public Employees - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm a state corrections officer — civil service rules give me an appeal to the personnel board with a 15-day deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm a state corrections officer — civil service gives me a personnel-board appeal with a 15-day deadline.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private logistics company — no civil service, no union, just their internal 'appeal' that goes nowhere.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Private company — no civil service or union, just their dead-end internal appeal.
scenarios.vetting_note: Public-employment status activates a separate procedural system with hard deadlines.

scenarios.opening_query: My paycheck situation at the restaurant is a mess and the owner laughs it off every time I ask.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: He keeps the card tips to 'cover breakage' and pays $2 something an hour claiming tip credit — servers at both his locations are in the same boat, and we want the federal case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He keeps our card tips for 'breakage' and pays two-something an hour on a tip credit — all servers at both locations. We want the federal case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether tip-credit violations support a collective federal claim
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just my own final check he's holding since I quit — three weeks of pay under state law.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my final check he's held for three weeks since I quit.
scenarios.vetting_note: Tip-pool violations vs final-paycheck claims; scale and statute differ though the opening is identical.

scenarios.opening_query: I have a wage claim against my old employer but there's a complication I didn't expect.
scenarios.initial: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee || Bankruptcy > Creditors
scenarios.hidden_fact: The company just filed Chapter 7 — I got a bankruptcy notice, and my unpaid wages are now a priority claim I have to file in that case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The company filed Chapter 7 — my unpaid wages are now a priority claim I have to file in the bankruptcy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employer is in bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Labor & Employment > Wage and Hour Claims - Employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The complication is they reincorporated under a new name — same owner, same shop — and claim the 'old company' owes me, not them.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They reincorporated under a new name — same owner, same shop — and say the 'old company' owes me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Employer bankruptcy converts a wage claim into creditor practice with strict deadlines.
